steps: Group the four terms into two pairs: $(12x^3 + 60x^2y) + (xy^2 + 5y^3)$.
Both groups share the binomial factor $(x + 5y)$; factor it out to get $(12x^2 + y^2)(x + 5y)$.
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$(12x^2 + 5y)(x + y^2)$': Expanding gives $12x^3 + 12x^2y^2 + 5xy + 5y^3$, which differs from the original. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(12x^2 + y^2)(x + 5y)$': Expanding gives $12x^3 + 60x^2y + xy^2 + 5y^3$, which matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — '$(12x^3 + y^2)(x^2 + 5y^3)$': Expanding gives $12x^5 + 60x^3y^3 + x^2y^2 + 5y^5$, wrong degrees throughout. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(12x^3 + y^3)(x + 5y)$': Expanding gives $12x^4 + 60x^3y + xy^3 + 5y^4$, wrong degrees. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
